LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER DAVID G. SUADI, D.O.'S MOTION TO STRIKE (PORTION OF RESPONDENT'S OPPOSITION TO APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION). RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION DETERMINING ISSUANCE OF MANDATORY INJUNCTION STAYING THE TERMINATION OF PETITIONER'S STATUS AS A PARTICIPATING PROVIDER UNDER THE INSURANCE ISSUED BY RESPONDENT. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE IMMEDIATE IRREPARABLE HARM OR THE RIGHT TO RELIEF UNDER POTVIN MET LIFE INSURANCE CO. (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 1060, 1071. IT ALSO DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS FOR THE BY-LAW IS CLEAR AND DEFENDANT WAS MERELY DOING WHAT THE PARTIES AGREED TO DO. IN ADDITION, THE COURT ADOPTED THE FOLLOWING BRIEFING SCHEDULE FOR THE HEARING ON THE ISSUANCE OF THE WRIT AS AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES: PETITIONER TO FILE OPENING BRIEF BY 01/30/09; BLUE SHIELD'S RESPONSE DUE 02/16/09; PETITIONER'S REPLY DUE 02/23/09; HEARING ON WRIT SET FOR 03/02/09. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: RHONDA AQUILINA, CSR 9956

LAW AND MOTION, 302, HEARING ON WRIT PER HEARING OF DECEMBER 19, 2008. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR, PER MOVING PARTY. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759

Hearing On Writ
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Hearing on Writ per hearing of December 19, 2008. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar, per moving party. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Motion Determining Issuance Of Mandatory Injunction Staying The Termination Ofpetitioner'S Status As A Participating Provider Under The Insurance Issued By Respondent
Off Calendar Dec-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Motion Determining Issuance Of Mandatory Injunction Staying The Termination Of petitioner's Status As A Participating Provider Under The Insurance Issued By Respondent.
APPEARANCES: Robert W. Stewart, Esq. of 479-3600 for petitioner & moving party; Lawrence J. Rose, Esq. 350-0403 for respondent & opposing party; (parties submit to tentative ruling).
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE IMMEDIATE IRREPARABLE HARM OR THE RIGHT TO RELIEF UNDER POTVIN MET LIFE INSURANCE CO. (2000) 22 CAL.4TH 1060, 1071. IT ALSO DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS FOR THE BY-LAW IS CLEAR AND DEFENDANT WAS MERELY DOING WHAT THE PARTIES AGREED TO DO. In addition, the Court adopted the following briefing schedule for the hearing on the issuance of the writ as agreed to by the parties: Petitioner to file opening brief by 01/30/09; Blue Shield's response due 02/16/09; petitioner's reply due 02/23/09; hearing on writ set for 03/02/09. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Rhonda Aquilina, CSR 9956
